label: Shumlak's m=0 sheared-flow stabilization is complementary, not competing — together both modes are closed
description: Shumlak & Hartman 1995 established that sheared axial flow above v'/(kV_A) ≈ 0.1 stabilizes the m=0 sausage in static Z-pinches; the ZaP experiment (Shumlak 2001) and FuZE (Zhang 2019) operationally demonstrate this in long-quiescent plasmas. Atlas explicitly notes that this result is silent on m=1, and that its own H3 ingredient closes the distinct m=1 obstruction. On RIGID-BELTRAMI-A both ingredients are simultaneously present, so the joint configuration potentially closes both modes. The mode families differ (m=0 for Shumlak, m=1 for Atlas), so this is a complementary rather than a generalizing result.

scope.regime: Z-pinch with sheared axial flow (Shumlak) + helical sheared flow with H3 (Atlas)
scope.framework: ideal MHD, two complementary mode-family analyses

contributing_claims.label: Sheared axial flow v'/(kV_A) > ~0.1 stabilizes m=0 sausage mode in static Z-pinch
ZaP experiment observes long stable period (>700× growth time) coincident with sheared sub-Alfvénic flow
Required shear for m=0 stabilization scales as ~c_0(ka)/(ka)·α with α≪1; m=1 needs different mechanism
Kadomtsev's interchange criterion has an entropy-gradient interpretation analogous to Schwarzschild-Ledoux
FuZE experimentally demonstrates sustained ~5μs neutron production during ~16μs quiescent period
Framework simultaneously closes five prior conjectures (Bondeson-Iacono helical, Hameiri Sturmian, n^-4 sharpness, SARI exclusion, kink obstruction)

contributing_evidence.label: m=0 stabilization at v'/(kV_A) ≈ 0.1 demonstrated numerically
ZaP stable period 15-20μs (>700× growth time) coincident with shear
m=0 shear-stabilization scaling du_z/dr ~ c_0/(ka)·α with α<<1
FuZE: ~5μs sustained neutron emission, (1.25±0.45)×10^5 n/pulse, scaling with n_D^2
Kadomtsev marginal-stable profile has entropy-gradient interpretation analogous to Schwarzschild-Ledoux

open_questions.question: Can RIGID-BELTRAMI-A-like profiles be realized on an existing ZaP/FuZE-class device?

suggested_next_steps.description: Design FuZE-mode discharge that achieves double-Beltrami Bz(r) with both axial and azimuthal shear
Couple Atlas's H1-H5 framework with Shumlak's m=0 shear-stabilization analysis; demonstrate joint stability on a common profile

open_questions.question: Does the combined m=0 + m=1 stabilization extrapolate to higher mode numbers (m≥2)?
open_questions.feeds_into: Atlas's own (m,k) grid scan covers m∈{1..4} but the m=1 theorem is specific

suggested_next_steps.description: Derive m-dependent generalization of the H3 quartic structure

transitions_out.relation: complementary — different mode family, joint stability achievable
